title: leucanthemopsis flaveola
intro: Leucanthemopsis flaveola, also known as the Yellow Hawk's Beard, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis flaveola include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. Leucanthemopsis flaveola is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it may also be used in topical creams or ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
